scope.objective: bruteforce solver/e2e behavior with timing, outcomes, failure flags, model-effectiveness signals

notes: Used API-driven solver trigger/control and endpoint probes.
Excluded flaky UI button path as requested.
Campaign resumed from existing checkpoint and completed full 25/25 cases.

section_4_non_consumable_pool_return.observed_pattern: post_district and post_available increase on return; state/national remain unchanged when source scope is district

available_from_campaign: solver completion under repeated demand waves
not_computed_in_this_backend_only_pass: UI priority-time spread plots from strict UI runner
section_6_model_effectiveness_signals.recommendation: Run run_stability_matrix.py after this dossier for deeper deterministic/model splits.

flagged.name: previous_ui_strict_mass_run
flagged.reason: backend read timeout (40s) plus UI/env availability issues

section_8_root_causes: Intermittent backend unavailability during long UI strict sessions caused aborts.
Heavy summary endpoints (/state|/national allocations/summary) are dominant latency contributors.
UI path added additional flake unrelated to solver correctness.
section_9_fixes_applied_or_workarounds: Switched to backend-only solver control as requested.
Used checkpoint-resume campaign to avoid losing progress.
Collected explicit endpoint latency matrix to isolate hot paths.
section_10_next_improvements: Add retry/backoff wrapper around /district/solver-status and summary endpoints in long runners.
Introduce paginated/limited summary fetch in state/national aggregation endpoints for large runs.
Keep UI strict runner for interaction audits only; use backend harness for throughput certification.
Generate per-run latency histogram and timeout budget thresholds in CI evidence.
